notes:
I've finished the initial concept for 'The Electric Curator.' 

This cinematic portfolio captures that high-octane Neo Tokyo energy through a combination of rain-slicked black backgrounds, electric cyan and magenta neon accents, and bilingual (English/Japanese) transit-inspired typography. 

The experience starts with a massive, atmospheric billboard and transitions into a series of project cards designed to feel like digital screens on a midnight city drive.

I've also set up a custom design system to maintain this specific 'Blade Runner' aesthetic across any future screens. How does this direction feel to you?
prompt: Design a cinematic art direction portfolio with Neo Tokyo and Blade Runner energy: rain-soaked black surfaces, cyan and magenta neon haze, bilingual transit-inspired typography, reflective chrome cues, animated project billboards, and a high-contrast scroll rhythm. Make the page feel like a premium midnight city drive rather than a generic cyberpunk collage.
suggestions: Add a project detail page with technical data overlays
Create a contact page with a 'Mission Control' theme
Try a more chrome-heavy look for the project cards
title: The Electric Curator | Homepage Archive
